violations.text: Working spray bottles/containers used for storing chemicals were not stored properly to prevent the possible contamination of clean equipment (hanging from wire shelving at/over clean equipment storage racks). Chemicals must be stored in a way to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. Spray bottles were relocated for proper storage at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

The garage door to the outside, located in the adjacent to the prep/cooking area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 02/23/2026

The pre-flush spray hose at the 3-bay sink was hanging below the flood rim. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. Sprayer hose spring was adjusted to allow for a proper air gap at the 3-bay sink at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

Corners of the dry storage and kitchen areas behind equipment were observed to have droppings and debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and to aid in the current pest control program. Cleaning began at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

The ice bagging area (considered area of prep) does not have a hand-washing sink that is conveniently located and/or readily accessible. Hand-washing sinks must be located to allow convenient use by employees in food preparation, food dispensing, and ware-washing areas. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2024

The door to the outside, located near the main kitchen area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2024

Insect control devices were observed above areas of prep and/or food equipment. Insect control devices must be installed in areas that do not allow for potential contamination of food and equipment. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2024

Several knives and food storage containers were observed with tape or an accumulation of tape adhesive and kept in a manner that did not prevent miscellaneous sources of contamination. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2024
The outside dumpsters were not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/19/2024

The drain plug for an outside dumpster is missing. Drains in receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables must have drain plugs in place. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2024

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored on racks within prep/kitchen areas. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/19/2024
